description: A formal status of criminal authority in the Soviet Union and post-Soviet states, denoting a professional criminal who follows specific traditions and holds elite status within organized crime and prison environments. Originating in the early 20th century, thieves in law are 'crowned' through rituals and tattoos by consensus of existing Vory. They operate within a code that includes rejecting work and assisting authorities, and are drawn from various nationalities across post-Soviet states.
